action.reasoning: Observation: the 6-epoch DataParallel run collapsed to near-random accuracy, while stderr shows many UNEXPECTED text-model keys when loading CLIP weights. Analysis: model.py uses CLIPVisionModel.from_pretrained on a full CLIP checkpoint, so the vision backbone was likely not loaded correctly in the new path; combined with aggressive weighted sampling/augmentation, this destroyed performance. The reliable baseline from iter 1 is still best. Decision: fix backbone loading by using CLIPModel and extracting vision_model weights properly, simplify training back to a stable ERM recipe with mild augmentation and standard shuffling, train a short 3-epoch recovery run, test predict locally, and submit eval immediately.
action.command:
python train.py --epochs 3 --batch_size 128 --lr 2e-5 --dropout 0.1 --num_workers 12 && python predict.py --data_path /test_data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/preds.json && python - <<'PY'
import json
p=json.load(open('/tmp/preds.json'))['predictions']
print(len(p), min(p), max(p))
PY
